description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Hanno City has announced multiple stages of administrative and operational reviews as part of fiscal year 2026 (Reiwa 8) budget planning.

From the August 2025 version (Phase 1) through the January 2026 version (Phase 4), leading to the final version, the city has published its review. Projects under review are categorized as: revision or continuation of user fee burden, scale reduction, suspension or termination, or postponement of implementation.

The city describes the situation as "the greatest fiscal crisis in the city's history," with tax and other revenue falling far short while expenditures continue to grow. As of Phase 3, even after these reviews, there remained a gap of over 1 billion yen in the budget.

For fiscal year 2026, through salary cuts for the mayor and staff, workforce reduction, suspension, termination, or reduction of many administrative operations, and use of temporary national grants, the city expects to barely achieve budget planning without relying on its fiscal adjustment fund. However, the final version incorporates public feedback and will make minimal withdrawals from the fiscal adjustment fund for social security-related expenses that had been scheduled for termination or suspension.

Review worksheets (PDF files) for each version are available on the city's official website.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/sogoseisakubu/zaiseika/kinkyuuzaiseitaisaku/12184.html
